title: Bitter Vietnamese Onion Dumplings
description: Tender and flavorful onion dumplings featuring a bitter taste and a glassy-jelly texture, suitable for a solo dinner. This dish can be enjoyed with a side of steamed vegetables or a simple green salad.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the flour and salt.
Gradually add in the chopped onions and mix until well combined.
Add in the crushed black cardamom and mix until the dough is smooth.
Knead the dough for 5-7 minutes until it becomes elastic.
Divide the dough into 12 equal pieces and shape each piece into a ball.
Flatten each ball into a disk shape.
Heat a large skillet over medium heat and add in the sesame oil.
Cook the dumplings for 3-4 minutes on each side until they are golden brown.
Brush the dumplings with the lacquer glaze and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Serve the dumplings hot with a side of steamed vegetables.
